name: SWAT+ Builder
author: Mohammad Galib
description: Build and calibrate SWAT+ hydrological models from a USGS gauge ID — with locked-benchmark calibration, claim governance, and auditable evidence bundles.
readmeContent:
# SWAT+ Builder

Build and calibrate SWAT+ hydrological models from a single USGS gauge ID, entirely in Python — no QGIS required. Every result carries an evidence bundle with explicit, machine-readable claim tiers.

## Installation

```bash
# Core
pip install swatplus-builder

# With GIS stack (required for real watershed builds)
pip install "swatplus-builder[gis]"

# Full stack
pip install "swatplus-builder[gis,hyriver,gridmet,soils,mcp]"
```

## MCP Server (13 tools)

```bash
pip install "swatplus-builder[mcp]"
swat mcp   # starts the stdio MCP server
```

Add to your MCP client config:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "swatplus-builder": {
      "command": "swat",
      "args": ["mcp"],
      "env": {
        "SWATPLUS_EXE": "/usr/local/bin/swatplus_exe",
        "SWATPLUS_BUILDER_ARTIFACTS": "/data/artifacts"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Tool Surface

**Tier 0 — Canonical governed workflow (2 tools):** `run_workflow` (background build → run → calibrate → verify → evidence bundle from one gauge ID), `workflow_status` (poll for completion)

**Tier 1 — Basin workflow (8 tools):** `build_project`, `run_basin`, `calibrate`, `propose_parameters`, `compare_runs`, `query_artifacts`, `diagnose_failure`, `validate`

**Tier 2 — Benchmark / readiness (3 tools):** `lock_benchmark`, `locked_calibrate`, `readiness_table`

## Claim Governance

Every run produces an evidence bundle with an explicit claim tier (`exploratory` → `diagnostic` → `research_grade` → `publication_grade`). The package — not the agent — decides what may be claimed, via runtime gates and locked-benchmark verification.

## Links

- [Documentation](https://ai-hydro.github.io/swatplus-builder/)
- [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/swatplus-builder/)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/AI-Hydro/swatplus-builder)
- [SKILL.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AI-Hydro/swatplus-builder/main/SKILL.md)
llmsInstallationContent:
## Installation Instructions

1. Install: `pip install "swatplus-builder[gis,mcp]"`
2. Verify: `swat version` and `swat health`
3. Start MCP server: `swat mcp` (stdio transport)
4. Add the MCP server config to your AI-Hydro settings with command `swat` and args `["mcp"]`.
5. Set environment variables: `SWATPLUS_EXE` (path to SWAT+ engine binary) and `SWATPLUS_BUILDER_ARTIFACTS` (output directory).
6. The SWAT+ engine binary is NOT a pip dependency — provide your own `swatplus_exe` on PATH or via `SWATPLUS_EXE`.
7. Load the agent skill: read `SKILL.md` from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AI-Hydro/swatplus-builder/main/SKILL.md before issuing any tool calls.

Optional extras: `gis` (WhiteboxTools + rasterio + geopandas, required for real builds), `hyriver` (USGS/NHDPlus retrieval), `gridmet` (weather forcing), `soils` (gNATSGO), `mcp` (agent server), `all` (everything).
